D9 Biology L5 Evaluate Reference

Mantis Shrimp — 12 Photoreceptor Channels

stomatopod eyes carry up to 12 spectral photoreceptor classes plus 4 polarisation types — yet behavioural tests show they don't outperform 3-cone humans at colour discrimination

The Caribbean mantis shrimp Neogonodactylus oerstedii has 12 narrow-band photoreceptors spanning 300-700 nm in its mid-band rows R1-R6. Naïve expectation: hyperspectral vision. But Thoen et al. 2014 showed actual colour discrimination is *worse* than human — the brain seems to use the channels as a fast spectral matched-filter rather than a full hyperspectral encoder.

The bar plot shows the 12 channel sensitivities. Each spike at a different λ_max — narrow Gaussians instead of the broad human L/M/S.
